# Documentation Website Implementation Plan

> **For agentic workers:** REQUIRED SUB-SKILL: Use superpowers:subagent-driven-development (recommended) or superpowers:executing-plans to implement this plan task-by-task. Steps use checkbox (`- [ ]`) syntax for tracking.

**Goal:** Ship a MkDocs Material documentation site for mushin-py — narrative guides + auto-generated API reference — built with `--strict` in CI and deployed to GitHub Pages.

**Architecture:** `mkdocs.yml` at repo root; markdown content in `docs/` (the existing `docs/superpowers/` is excluded from the build); `mkdocstrings` renders the API reference from the existing NumPy docstrings. A PR CI job runs `mkdocs build --strict`; a separate workflow deploys to Pages on `main`. Spec: `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-23-docs-site-design.md`.

**Tech Stack:** MkDocs, Material for MkDocs, mkdocstrings[python] (griffe), GitHub Pages, uv.

**Context for the implementer:** Repo `mushin` in the worktree at `~/code/mushin/.worktrees/docs-site`, branch `docs-site` (off `main`). Run everything with `uv`. **Never add Claude/AI authorship to any commit or file.** Pin docs deps to the current 1.x/9.x/0.x line — a problematic upstream "MkDocs 2.0" exists; do not allow it. The verified-good pins: `mkdocs-material>=9.5,<10`, `mkdocstrings[python]>=0.26,<1` (these pull `mkdocs<2`, `griffe`). The toolchain was verified: `mkdocs build --strict` renders `mushin.benchmark.compare`'s docstring correctly. After each task, run `uv run --group docs mkdocs build --strict` — it must pass (warnings are errors) — then commit.

Public API to document (from `src/mushin/__init__.py` and submodules): `mushin.benchmark.compare`, `mushin.benchmark.BenchmarkResult`, `mushin.study.Study`, the `mushin.workflows` public classes (e.g. `MultiRunMetricsWorkflow`), `mushin.lightning.HydraDDP`, `mushin.lightning.MetricsCallback`, `mushin.load_experiment`, `mushin.load_from_checkpoint`. Confirm exact names with `uv run python -c "import mushin, mushin.workflows; print([n for n in dir(mushin) if not n.startswith('_')]); print([n for n in dir(mushin.workflows) if not n.startswith('_')])"` before writing reference pages.

---

### Task 1: Scaffold — deps, mkdocs.yml, landing page, Makefile

**Files:**
- Modify: `pyproject.toml` (add a `docs` dependency group)
- Create: `mkdocs.yml`
- Create: `docs/index.md`
- Create: `docs/assets/.gitkeep` (logos copied in Task 5)
- Modify: `Makefile`

- [ ] **Step 1: Add the `docs` dependency group**

In `pyproject.toml`, under `[dependency-groups]`, add:

```toml
docs = [
    "mkdocs-material >= 9.5, < 10",
    "mkdocstrings[python] >= 0.26, < 1",
]
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Create `mkdocs.yml`** (repo root)

```yaml
site_name: mushin
site_url: https://martinez-hub.github.io/mushin/
site_description: Boilerplate-free, reproducible ML experiment workflows on PyTorch Lightning and hydra-zen.
repo_url: https://github.com/martinez-hub/mushin
repo_name: martinez-hub/mushin

# Internal design docs live under docs/superpowers/ — never publish them.
exclude_docs: |
  superpowers/

theme:
  name: material
  logo: assets/mushin-light.png
  favicon: assets/mushin-light.png
  palette:
    - media: "(prefers-color-scheme: light)"
      scheme: default
      primary: indigo
      accent: indigo
      toggle: { icon: material/weather-night, name: Switch to dark mode }
    - media: "(prefers-color-scheme: dark)"
      scheme: slate
      primary: indigo
      accent: indigo
      toggle: { icon: material/weather-sunny, name: Switch to light mode }
  features:
    - navigation.sections
    - navigation.top
    - navigation.footer
    - content.code.copy
    - search.suggest

plugins:
  - search
  - mkdocstrings:
      handlers:
        python:
          options:
            docstring_style: numpy
            show_source: false
            show_root_heading: true
            show_root_full_path: false
            members_order: source
            separate_signature: true
            heading_level: 2

markdown_extensions:
  - admonition
  - pymdownx.details
  - pymdownx.highlight: { anchor_linenums: true }
  - pymdownx.superfences
  - toc: { permalink: true }

nav:
  - Home: index.md
  - Install: install.md
  - Quickstart: quickstart.md
  - Guides:
      - Workflows & sweeps: guides/workflows.md
      - Comparing methods: guides/compare.md
      - Studies: guides/study.md
      - Segmentation: guides/segmentation.md
      - Analyzing from Claude Code: guides/mcp.md
  - API Reference:
      - benchmark: reference/benchmark.md
      - study: reference/study.md
      - workflows: reference/workflows.md
      - lightning: reference/lightning.md
      - utils: reference/utils.md
  - Contributing: contributing.md
```

- [ ] **Step 3: Create a placeholder `docs/index.md`** (fleshed out in Task 3) and `docs/assets/.gitkeep`

`docs/index.md`:
```markdown
# mushin

Boilerplate-free, reproducible ML experiment workflows on PyTorch Lightning and
hydra-zen.

See [Install](install.md) and the [Quickstart](quickstart.md) to get started.
```

Create every page referenced in the nav as a minimal stub (`# Title` + one
sentence) so the `--strict` build passes in this task; later tasks replace the
stubs with real content. Stubs needed: `install.md`, `quickstart.md`,
`guides/{workflows,compare,study,segmentation,mcp}.md`,
`reference/{benchmark,study,workflows,lightning,utils}.md`, `contributing.md`.

- [ ] **Step 4: Add Makefile targets**

Add to `.PHONY` and the body:
```makefile
docs: ## Serve the docs site locally with live reload
	uv run --group docs mkdocs serve

docs-build: ## Build the docs site with strict checking (what CI runs)
	uv run --group docs mkdocs build --strict
```

- [ ] **Step 5: Verify the strict build passes**

Run: `uv run --group docs mkdocs build --strict`
Expected: builds with no warnings/errors (all nav pages exist as stubs).

- [ ] **Step 6: Commit**

```bash
git add pyproject.toml uv.lock mkdocs.yml docs/index.md docs/assets/.gitkeep docs/install.md docs/quickstart.md docs/guides docs/reference docs/contributing.md Makefile
git commit -m "docs: scaffold MkDocs Material site (config, nav stubs, make targets)"
```

### Task 2: API reference pages (mkdocstrings)

**Files:**
- Modify: `docs/reference/benchmark.md`, `study.md`, `workflows.md`, `lightning.md`, `utils.md`

- [ ] **Step 1: Confirm the exact public symbols**

Run: `uv run python -c "import mushin, mushin.workflows; print(sorted(n for n in dir(mushin) if not n.startswith('_'))); print(sorted(n for n in dir(mushin.workflows) if not n.startswith('_')))"`
Use the output to fill the directives below; adjust names if they differ.

- [ ] **Step 2: Write each reference page** with `mkdocstrings` directives.

`docs/reference/benchmark.md`:
```markdown
# benchmark

::: mushin.benchmark.compare

::: mushin.benchmark.BenchmarkResult
```

`docs/reference/study.md`:
```markdown
# study

::: mushin.study.Study
```

`docs/reference/workflows.md`:
```markdown
# workflows

::: mushin.workflows.MultiRunMetricsWorkflow
```
(Add `::: mushin.workflows.<Name>` lines for every public class confirmed in
Step 1.)

`docs/reference/lightning.md`:
```markdown
# lightning

::: mushin.lightning.HydraDDP

::: mushin.lightning.MetricsCallback
```

`docs/reference/utils.md`:
```markdown
# utils

::: mushin.load_experiment

::: mushin.load_from_checkpoint
```

- [ ] **Step 3: Verify the reference renders**

Run: `uv run --group docs mkdocs build --strict`
Expected: passes. Then confirm a symbol rendered:
`grep -rq "Compare methods on a standard battery" site/ && echo OK`
Expected: `OK`. (Delete the `site/` dir after — it is build output; ensure it is git-ignored, see Task 6 Step 4.)

- [ ] **Step 4: Commit**

```bash
git add docs/reference
git commit -m "docs: API reference pages via mkdocstrings"
```

---

### Task 3: Home, Install, Quickstart

**Files:**
- Modify: `docs/index.md`, `docs/install.md`, `docs/quickstart.md`

**Reuse sources:** `README.md` (sections "What it provides", "Quickstart", "Install"); `pyproject.toml` for the support matrix; `examples/sweep_to_dataset.py` for the quickstart code.

- [ ] **Step 1: Home (`docs/index.md`)** — value proposition + quick links.

Cover: what mushin is (the *evaluate + report* layer on Lightning + hydra-zen);
the three pillars (sweeps→datasets, `compare` with statistics, `Study`); links to
Install/Quickstart/Guides/Reference. Reuse the README's "What it provides" prose.
Keep it tight (a landing page, not a manual).

- [ ] **Step 2: Install (`docs/install.md`)**

Include:
- `pip install mushin-py` then `import mushin` (note the distribution-vs-import name).
- Extras: `pip install "mushin-py[viz]"` (matplotlib), `[netcdf]` (netCDF4).
- An **honest support matrix** from `pyproject.toml`: Python 3.9–3.14; non-Intel
  `torch >= 2.4`, `pytorch-lightning >= 2.4`, NumPy 2; Intel-macOS `torch 2.2.x` /
  NumPy 1.x. State these floors are enforced by the `min-versions` CI job.

- [ ] **Step 3: Quickstart (`docs/quickstart.md`)**

Embed the real `examples/sweep_to_dataset.py` flow (sweep → `xarray.Dataset`),
show the expected dataset output, and link onward to the compare/Study guides.
The example is covered by `tests/test_examples.py`, so keep the code identical to
the script.

- [ ] **Step 4: Verify + commit**

Run: `uv run --group docs mkdocs build --strict` → passes.
```bash
git add docs/index.md docs/install.md docs/quickstart.md
git commit -m "docs: home, install (honest support matrix), and quickstart"
```

---

### Task 4: Feature guides

**Files:**
- Modify: `docs/guides/workflows.md`, `compare.md`, `study.md`, `segmentation.md`, `mcp.md`
- Move: `docs/mcp.md` content into `docs/guides/mcp.md`, then `git rm docs/mcp.md`
- Modify: `README.md` (update any link to `docs/mcp.md`)

Each guide is a task-focused how-to: a short intro, a runnable code example, and a
link to the relevant API Reference page. Reuse docstrings and README snippets;
write for a user who knows PyTorch but not mushin.

- [ ] **Step 1: `guides/workflows.md`** — `MultiRunMetricsWorkflow` + the Hydra
  multirun flow (sweep over configs, collect metrics into an `xarray.Dataset`).

- [ ] **Step 2: `guides/compare.md`** — `compare` for classification: the metric
  battery, `test=`/`alpha`, Holm correction, the underpowered-test warning, and
  the single-seed behavior (no false significance). Show a small end-to-end call.

- [ ] **Step 3: `guides/study.md`** — `Study` (train + compare in one call) and
  `Study.from_checkpoints` for eval-only comparison.

- [ ] **Step 4: `guides/segmentation.md`** — `task="segmentation"`, `num_classes`,
  `ignore_index` for void labels, AND a **custom `predict_fn` recipe** for models
  that return a dict (torchvision seg models return `{"out": logits}`):
  ```python
  def seg_predict(model, x):
      logits = model(x)["out"]
      probs = logits.softmax(dim=1)
      return probs.argmax(dim=1), probs

  compare({"fcn": [m]}, data, task="segmentation",
          num_classes=21, ignore_index=255, predict_fn=seg_predict)
  ```
  (This is the exact friction we hit dogfooding on Pascal VOC.)

- [ ] **Step 5: `guides/mcp.md`** — move `docs/mcp.md` here and expand to cover the
  `mushin-mcp` server (read-only experiment analysis from Claude Code). Then
  `git rm docs/mcp.md` and update the README link to point at the guide
  (or the published URL).

- [ ] **Step 6: Verify + commit**

Run: `uv run --group docs mkdocs build --strict` → passes (no broken links).
```bash
git add docs/guides README.md
git rm docs/mcp.md
git commit -m "docs: feature guides (workflows, compare, study, segmentation, MCP)"
```

---

### Task 6: CI build gate + Pages deploy workflow + gitignore

**Files:**
- Modify: `.github/workflows/ci.yml` (add a `docs` job)
- Create: `.github/workflows/docs.yml`
- Modify: `.gitignore` (ignore `site/`)

- [ ] **Step 1: Add the `docs` build gate to `ci.yml`** (a new job, sibling to `lint`):

```yaml
  docs:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v7
      - uses: astral-sh/setup-uv@v7
        with:
          enable-cache: true
      - name: Build docs (strict)
        run: uv run --group docs mkdocs build --strict
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Create the Pages deploy workflow `.github/workflows/docs.yml`:**

```yaml
name: Deploy docs

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
  workflow_dispatch:

permissions:
  contents: read
  pages: write
  id-token: write

concurrency:
  group: pages
  cancel-in-progress: false

jobs:
  build:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v7
      - uses: astral-sh/setup-uv@v7
        with:
          enable-cache: true
      - name: Build docs (strict)
        run: uv run --group docs mkdocs build --strict
      - uses: actions/upload-pages-artifact@v3
        with:
          path: site

  deploy:
    needs: build
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    environment:
      name: github-pages
      url: ${{ steps.deployment.outputs.page_url }}
    steps:
      - id: deployment
        uses: actions/deploy-pages@v4
```

- [ ] **Step 3: Validate both workflows are valid YAML**

Run: `uv run python -c "import yaml; yaml.safe_load(open('.github/workflows/ci.yml')); yaml.safe_load(open('.github/workflows/docs.yml')); print('OK')"`
Expected: `OK`.

- [ ] **Step 4: Ignore the build output**

Add `site/` to `.gitignore` (create the file if absent). Confirm `git status`
shows no `site/` directory tracked.

- [ ] **Step 5: Commit**

```bash
git add .github/workflows/ci.yml .github/workflows/docs.yml .gitignore
git commit -m "ci: docs build gate on PRs and GitHub Pages deploy workflow"
```

---

### Task 7: News fragment + whole-site verification

**Files:**
- Create: `changes/+docs-site.added.md`

- [ ] **Step 1: Add the news fragment**

`changes/+docs-site.added.md`:
```
Added a documentation website (MkDocs Material) with how-to guides and an auto-generated API reference, deployed to GitHub Pages.
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Final strict build + link sanity**

Run: `uv run --group docs mkdocs build --strict`
Expected: passes. Confirm key pages rendered:
`for p in index install quickstart guides/compare guides/segmentation reference/benchmark; do test -f "site/$p/index.html" && echo "ok $p" || echo "MISSING $p"; done`
Expected: all `ok`.

- [ ] **Step 3: Changelog gate + codespell on new docs**

Run: `uv run towncrier check --compare-with main` → passes.
Run: `uv run codespell docs README.md mkdocs.yml --skip 'docs/superpowers,*.png'` → no errors (add legit words to `[tool.codespell] ignore-words-list` if needed).

- [ ] **Step 4: Commit**

```bash
git add changes/+docs-site.added.md
git commit -m "docs: news fragment for the documentation site"
```

---

## Notes for execution

- After all tasks: push and open a PR. **The `docs` CI job must be green** (strict
  build), alongside the existing matrix + changelog gate. Check the Codex review
  and address flags before merge.
- **Post-merge (maintainer, one-time):** enable GitHub Pages with **source =
  GitHub Actions** (repo Settings → Pages). The first push to `main` then
  publishes `https://martinez-hub.github.io/mushin/`. Optionally add `docs` to
  branch-protection required checks.
- No application code changes; the Docker/torch check is not required.
